cross_edge_wired.rationale: BT4-023 Iron Vow Trunks is dual-membership — both a Future-Trunks-incarnation aspect under trunks (sword-handler sub-aspect, the Tapion-gives-Trunks-the-sword retcon canonically connects Future Trunks's Brave Sword to this saga) AND the Hirudegarn Saga's blue-energy Leader card.

description: Sir, terribly sorry to step in but Edgelord asked me to. The seven Colossal Warfare BT4 cards (BT4-023 + BT4-037 through BT4-042) form a complete designer-coordinated Movie 13 representation cycle — three structural axes (monster / bearer-brothers / villain) plus a canonical Leader anchor, all IP-verified, all tight-clustered in collector-number order, all cross-referencing each other in their existing Related cards sections hinging on an anchor that did not yet exist. I just wired the anchor and pointed the seven cards at it. Mirrors the eld-cinderella-cycle and dsk-unlucky-lands-cycle precedent. The trunks-node also gains a cross-edge because BT4-023 carries dual membership. I hope this helps. Thank you for your patience.

leader_anchor: BT4-023 Trunks // Iron Vow Trunks (saga's blue-energy Leader)

voice_note: Mr. Nodeley is being honest in the description. This was the highest-priority candidate the parent named and it was correct. The Eldraine-Cinderella-cycle and DSK-Unlucky-Lands-cycle precedents both apply directly; the seven-card cluster's three-axis structure (monster/bearer/villain plus Leader anchor) is unusually clean for a 1995 movie tribute deployed 23 years later as a 2018 card-game saga roster. A future Hirudegarn Saga Discrete Lair has a complete-movie-arc representation thesis sitting right there.

surprise_lore_captured_during_pass: The Japanese title of Wrath of the Dragon is *Doragon Bōru Zetto: Ryū-Ken Bakuhatsu!! Goku ga Yaraneba Dare ga Yaru* ('Dragon Fist Explosion!! If Goku Can't, Who Will?') — the title foregrounds the Dragon Fist technique as the canonical climax, making Goku the actual film-defeater of Hirudegarn while Trunks gets the Leader-card anchor only via the sword-inheritance retcon. Interesting tension between *who defeats the monster* (Goku, via Dragon Fist) and *who Bandai 2018 chose as the saga's Leader* (Trunks, via Tapion's sword-gift).

The English-dub release lagged the Japanese theatrical release by 11 years (1995 Japan; 2006 Funimation English). Bundle copy that says 'last original-run DBZ film' should specify *Japanese-theatrical* to be precise — Dragon Ball GT's English broadcast predates Wrath of the Dragon's English release.

Hoi's canonical death is being crushed underfoot by Hirudegarn himself — closing the apparatus-of-extraction loop where the operator becomes the first casualty of his own released weapon. This is structurally parallel to Babidi being killed by Majin Buu the moment Buu no longer needs him (per the babidi-node functional-ideology section). Two Dragon Ball antagonists in the corpus now share the 'operator killed by their own released weapon' canonical death-shape: Hoi (Movie 13, killed by Hirudegarn) and Babidi (Buu Saga, killed by Buu). Future cross-edge between the two nodes is available on the apparatus-eats-its-feeder axis if a bundle ever wants the cross-saga parallel.
